title: Spare Parts (short story)

"'''Spare Parts'''" is a short story written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] that was originally published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]] by [[West End Games]] in [[November]] [[1996]] and was later archived online at [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] on [[March 25]], [[2005]].

==Plot summary==
A pair of [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] accidentally steal one of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[CZ-3/Legends|business droids]], but it wanders free from their care. They search the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] for it, narrowly avoiding the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] attack that torches [[Jek Nkik]]'s [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]]. In the end, the droid theft is a non-issue – the droid was deliberately set up to be stolen in a plot devised by Jabba to catch a business rival red-handed. 

==Continuity==
The [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] named [[Macemillian-winduarté]] – Mace Windu for short – first saw print in ''[[Galaxy Guide 7: Mos Eisley]]'' ([[1993]]). Therefore, this "Mace Windu" was established as a character years before the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] seen in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]], the result of the creators of the Squib character picking a name from an earlier ''[[Star Wars]]'' draft script written by [[George Lucas]].<ref name="TERC" />
